pr.title: Match 5 functions: lbRefract, lbbgflash, ftCo, ftAnim
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Closed, unmerged PR that attempted to match five small functions across library/background flash, refract graphics state, fighter common collision/physics, and fighter animation cleanup code. The implementations replaced placeholder markers for fn_8002087C, lbRefract_800225D4, ftCo_Rebirth_Coll, ftCo_CatchDash_Phys, and ftAnim_80070F28, plus tightened lbbgflash prototypes and introduced local BgFlashState/BgFlashData layout needed by fn_8002087C. No review comments were present in the slice, so acceptance rationale or closure reason is not evidenced.
what_changed: Implemented ftCo_CatchDash_Phys in ftCo_Attack100.c by reading Fighter from gobj->user_data and calling ft_80085030 with common-data x64 scaled by the fighter's ground friction and facing direction.
Implemented ftCo_Rebirth_Coll in ft_0D31.c by GET_FIGHTER lookup, checking fp->smash_attrs.x2135 == -1, and calling ft_80083DCC when that condition holds.
Added include of ft/ft_081B.h in ft_0D31.c to support the new rebirth collision implementation.

Implemented ftAnim_80070F28 in ftanim.c as a cleanup loop over fp->x8B0 slots: for active slots where x11 is not -1, it uses ft_data->x1C[i] part lists to clear fp->parts[part_idx].flags_b5 and then resets slot->x11 to -1.

Implemented fn_8002087C in lbbgflash.c by defining local BgFlashState and BgFlashData structs, declaring extern BgFlashData lbl_80433658, clearing state.active, setting state.mode to 5, and storing *arg0 into xC.
Updated lbbgflash.h prototypes for fn_8002087C from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void fn_8002087C(int* arg0), and for lbBgFlash_80021A10 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void lbBgFlash_80021A10(f32).

Implemented lbRefract_800225D4 in lbrefract.c as a graphics-state reset helper calling GXSetTevDirect(0), GXSetNumIndStages(0), and HSD_StateInvalidate(-1), adding baselib/state.h for HSD_StateInvalidate.
smart_moves: Converted unknown lbbgflash declarations to specific prototypes once the implementation made argument and return behavior clear.
Used a narrow local struct reconstruction for lbl_80433658 instead of over-expanding global header exposure, which kept the change focused on fields needed by fn_8002087C.
For ftAnim_80070F28, relied on ARRAY_SIZE(fp->x8B0) rather than a hardcoded slot count, making the loop more robust to existing struct definitions.
The ftAnim implementation preserves signed sentinel semantics by testing (s8)slot->x11 != -1 before clearing per-part flags and resetting x11.
The lbRefract match recognized the function as a compact GX/HSD render-state reset, requiring only the correct baselib state include and exact call sequence.
decomp_lessons: Small placeholder functions often become matchable after nearby data layout is minimally reconstructed; fn_8002087C needed bitfield state fields and xC in BgFlashData.

When matching fighter status callbacks, check whether existing engine helpers already encode common physics or collision behavior; ftCo_CatchDash_Phys delegates to ft_80085030, while ftCo_Rebirth_Coll delegates to ft_80083DCC under a single attribute condition.

Animation cleanup functions may use fighter data tables to translate logical slots into part indices; ftAnim_80070F28 shows the pattern fp->ft_data->x1C[i] -> data->x4 parts list -> fp->parts[part_idx].flags_b5.
Header cleanups should accompany matched functions when UNK_PARAMS can be replaced by concrete argument types, but only for symbols whose signatures are evidenced by the implementation.
Graphics decomp work should watch for short reset routines that pair Dolphin GX calls with HSD_StateInvalidate to mark cached render state dirty.
naming_conventions: Existing address-based symbol names were retained for unknown or not-yet-renamed functions, such as fn_8002087C, lbRefract_800225D4, ftAnim_80070F28, and lbBgFlash_80021A10.
Temporary reconstructed data types used subsystem-prefixed descriptive names: BgFlashState and BgFlashData.
Fighter callbacks followed existing status naming style, including ftCo_CatchDash_Phys and ftCo_Rebirth_Coll.
The code used established project access idioms such as Fighter_GObj, HSD_GObj, GET_FIGHTER(gobj), gobj->user_data, and fp for Fighter pointers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used bitfields in BgFlashState, with u8 active : 1 and u8 mode : 7, to represent packed background flash state writes.
Used explicit signed-byte comparison, (s8)slot->x11 != -1, to preserve sentinel behavior in ftAnim_80070F28.

Used the float-punning expression *(float*)&p_ftCommonData->x64 in ftCo_CatchDash_Phys, suggesting the underlying common-data field was not yet typed as f32 but the match required float interpretation.
Kept function bodies minimal and call-oriented where the assembly likely represented simple wrappers around existing helpers or GX/HSD state APIs.
Added only the includes needed for newly referenced symbols, such as ft/ft_081B.h and baselib/state.h.
review_feedback: No review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed without being merged, but the provided context does not include a human explanation for closure.
follow_up_queries: Find later merged PRs touching fn_8002087C or lbbgflash BgFlashData to verify whether this local struct layout was accepted or revised.
Search for later implementations of ftAnim_80070F28 to compare sentinel casts and part flag clearing logic.
Check whether lbRefract_800225D4 was later merged with the same GXSetTevDirect/GXSetNumIndStages/HSD_StateInvalidate sequence.
Investigate PR comments or commit history outside this slice for why PR #2014 was closed unmerged.
Search for p_ftCommonData->x64 typing work to see whether the float-punning expression in ftCo_CatchDash_Phys was later replaced by a typed f32 field.
